langgraph.notes: The answer includes all required facts (28 days, carry over up to 5 days, use by March 31) and supports the factual claims with cited sources.
langgraph.answer:
Full-time employees get 28 vacation days per calendar year [faq/employee_faq.md § How many vacation days do I get per year?] [policies/vacation_policy.md § Annual Leave Entitlement].

If you don’t use them all, you can carry over up to 5 days into the next calendar year [faq/employee_faq.md § Can I carry unused vacation days into the next year?] [policies/vacation_policy.md § Carry-Over Rule].

Those carried-over days must be used by March 31 of the new year; after that, they expire without compensation [faq/employee_faq.md § Can I carry unused vacation days into the next year?] [policies/vacation_policy.md § Carry-Over Rule].

Also, any unused days above the 5-day carry-over limit are forfeited at year end [policies/vacation_policy.md § Carry-Over Rule].

pydantic_ai.answer:
Full-time employees get 28 vacation days per calendar year [policies/vacation_policy.md § Annual Leave Entitlement] [faq/employee_faq.md § How many vacation days do I get per year?].

If you do not use them all, you can carry over up to 5 unused days into the next calendar year [policies/vacation_policy.md § Carry-Over Rule] [faq/employee_faq.md § Can I carry unused vacation days into the next year?].

Those carried-over days must be used by March 31 of the new year, otherwise they expire without compensation [policies/vacation_policy.md § Carry-Over Rule] [faq/employee_faq.md § Can I carry unused vacation days into the next year?].

Part-time staff receive a pro-rated entitlement [policies/vacation_policy.md § Annual Leave Entitlement] [faq/employee_faq.md § How many vacation days do I get per year?].

langgraph.notes: The answer states the required 30-day return window after delivery and supports the claims with cited policy/FAQ sources.
langgraph.answer: Customers can return an item for a refund within 30 days from the delivery date, not the order date [policies/refund_policy.md § Return Window] [faq/customer_faq.md § What is your return window?]. For change-of-mind returns, the same 30-day window applies [policies/refund_policy.md § Change-of-Mind Returns].

pydantic_ai.notes: The answer includes the required 30-day return window and supports its factual claims with cited policy/FAQ sources.
pydantic_ai.answer: Customers can return an item for a refund within 30 days from the delivery date, and the carrier-confirmed delivery date is the authoritative start of that window [policies/refund_policy.md § Return Window]. The FAQ states the same: returns are allowed within 30 days of the delivery date, not the order date [faq/customer_faq.md § What is your return window?].

langgraph.notes: The answer includes full refund and shipping coverage for damaged items, with all factual claims supported by cited policy/FAQ sections.
langgraph.answer:
Under ACME’s policy, if an item arrives damaged or defective, the customer is entitled to a full refund including shipping costs [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]. They do not pay return shipping; a prepaid return label is issued [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]. If they prefer, a replacement may be offered at no additional cost, subject to stock availability [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items].

Customers should contact support with their order number and, if possible, photos of the damage [faq/customer_faq.md § My item arrived damaged or defective. What happens now?]. Support agents are instructed to ask for photos of the damage and packaging where practical, attach them to the ticket, and record the damage report against the order [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items].

pydantic_ai.notes: The answer includes the required full refund and shipping entitlement for a damaged rag, and all stated policy details are accompanied by citations.
pydantic_ai.answer:
Under ACME policy, a customer whose item arrived damaged is entitled to:

- a **full refund, including shipping costs** [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]
- **no return shipping charge**; ACME issues a **prepaid return label** [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]
- potentially a **replacement at no additional cost**, if they prefer that and stock is available [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]

They should contact support with the **order number** and, where practical, **photos of the damage and packaging** [faq/customer_faq.md § My item arrived damaged or defective. What happens now?] [policies/refund_policy.md § Damaged or Defective Items]

langgraph.notes: The answer includes the €500 threshold and supervisor approval, with cited support for all factual claims.
langgraph.answer:
Refunds over €500 need extra approval, and that approval must come from a supervisor [policies/refund_policy.md § Approval Thresholds]. The same threshold is confirmed in the customer FAQ: “Refunds over €500 require supervisor approval before they can be processed” [faq/customer_faq.md § Why is my larger refund taking a little longer to approve?].

Also, any deviation from policy, such as goodwill refunds or out-of-window exceptions, requires supervisor approval regardless of amount [policies/refund_policy.md § Approval Thresholds].

pydantic_ai.notes: The answer states refunds over €500 require approval by a supervisor and cites the policy for its factual claims.
pydantic_ai.answer: Refunds **over €500** need extra approval, and the approver must be a **supervisor** [policies/refund_policy.md § Approval Thresholds]. The same section also says **any deviation from policy** (for example, goodwill or out-of-window refunds) requires supervisor approval **regardless of amount** [policies/refund_policy.md § Approval Thresholds].

langgraph.notes: The answer includes all required prices (4.95, 9.95, 12.95) with shipping times and cites sources for each factual claim.
langgraph.answer:
ACME offers three shipping options:

- Standard shipping within Germany: 3–5 business days, €4.95. It’s free for orders over €50 after discounts, before shipping [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates] [policies/shipping_policy.md § Free-Shipping Threshold]
- Express shipping within Germany: 1–2 business days, €9.95 [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
- EU shipping to other EU member states: 5–8 business days, €12.95 [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates] [faq/customer_faq.md § Do you ship to other EU countries?]

Timing details:
- Delivery times are in business days, Monday to Friday, excluding German public holidays, and start when the parcel is handed to the carrier, not from the order date [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
- Orders placed before 14:00 CET on a business day are normally handed to the carrier the same day [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]

pydantic_ai.notes: The answer includes all expected prices (4.95, 9.95, 12.95), matching shipping options, costs, times, and cites the shipping policy for each factual claim.
pydantic_ai.answer:
We offer three shipping options:

- **Standard shipping (Germany):** **€4.95**, or **free for orders over €50**; delivery in **3–5 business days** [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
- **Express shipping (Germany):** **€9.95**; delivery in **1–2 business days** [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
- **EU shipping (other EU member states):** **€12.95**; delivery in **5–8 business days** [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]

Timing notes:
- Delivery times are measured in **business days only (Monday–Friday, excluding German public holidays)** and start **from when the parcel is handed to the carrier, not the order date** [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
- Orders placed **before 14:00 CET on a business day** are **normally handed to the carrier the same day** [policies/shipping_policy.md § Shipping Options and Rates]
